content.problemStatement:
An entire class of students has gone to the ATM to withdraw cash. Let's name them {S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SN}\{S_1, S_2, \dots, S_N\}{S1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äč}. Obviously, they have to stand in a queue. They want to do so, such that close friends are standing not too far from each other. Specifically, if SiS_iSi‚Äč and SjS_jSj‚Äč are close friends, then there should not be more than 2 other students standing in between them. Given the close-friendship information, you need to tell whether it is possible to form such a queue, or not.

Formally, you should state whether there exists a permutation (Sp1,Sp2,‚Ä¶,SpN)(S_{p1}, S_{p2}, \dots, S_{pN})(Sp1‚Äč,Sp2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SpN‚Äč) of {S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SN}\{S_1, S_2, \dots, S_N\}{S1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äč}, such that for all pairs (Spi,Spj)(S_{pi}, S_{pj})(Spi‚Äč,Spj‚Äč) where SpiS_{pi}Spi‚Äč and SpjS_{pj}Spj‚Äč are close friends, ‚ą£i‚ąíj‚ą£‚Č§3|i-j| \leq 3‚ą£i‚ąíj‚ą£‚Č§3 holds true.


Input Format
The first line of input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.


The first line contains two integers N,MN, MN,M, denoting the number of students, and the number of close-friendship pairs, respectively.


The next MMM lines contain two integers each, separated by single spaces: i ji\ ji j, denoting that SiS_iSi‚Äč and SjS_jSj‚Äč are close friends.



Output Format
Output a single line for each test case, containing the answer, which should be 111, if such a queue is possible, and 000 otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Consider the permutation (S1,S3,S2,S4,S5)(S_1, S_3, S_2, S_4, S_5)(S1‚Äč,S3‚Äč,S2‚Äč,S4‚Äč,S5‚Äč). No pair of close friends have more than 2 students between them. So this is a valid queue, and hence the answer is 111.
